title: Today's AI Brief: Flush With Cash From OpenAI, Opal Is Making an AI-Powered Audio Gadget
summary: A San Francisco startup gets a cash infusion from OpenAI to make AI-powered audio gadgets, while Endava uses Codex to accelerate software delivery.
imageAlt: Image from source article for Flush With Cash From OpenAI, Opal Is Making an AI-Powered Audio Gadget
imageCaption: Flush With Cash From OpenAI, Opal Is Making an AI-Powered Audio Gadget
imageSourceTitle: Flush With Cash From OpenAI, Opal Is Making an AI-Powered Audio Gadget

body.text: I'm Leo Stone, and I'm here to walk you through some fresh news in the world of AI. First up, a small San Francisco startup called Opal Camera is rebranding to Opal Electronics and making a big push into AI-powered audio gadgets. They've just received a cash infusion from OpenAI, the company behind ChatGPT. This move caught my attention - who wouldn't want to see more AI innovation in the audio space?

According to Wired, Opal Electronics is looking to become the AI hardware company of the moment. They've made a name for themselves with well-designed webcams, and now they're expanding into AI-powered audio. It's interesting to see them focus on audio - we've seen a lot of AI advancements in the visual space, but audio is definitely an area that's ripe for innovation. I mean, think about it - how often do you use voice commands or listen to AI-generated podcasts?

In other news, Endava is using Codex to build what's called an 'agentic organization'. Essentially, they're using AI to accelerate software delivery and reduce the time it takes to analyze requirements from weeks to just hours. The potential for AI to really change the way companies work is huge - it's not just about automating tasks, but about fundamentally changing the way software is developed. I'm curious to see how this kind of technology will shape the industry.

My quick read: the artificial intelligence story keeps getting bigger, but also stranger in very human ways. One moment it is strategy and infrastructure; the next, the Vatican is in the room. That is a pretty good sign this technology has left the lab and wandered into the town square.
